text:
1. Tantra Vidya: General Concepts
• Tantra Vidya is mentioned as having ancient texts with detailed descriptions of
Vashikaran (attraction/control) and Mohini Vidya (enchantment/fascination).
• It is described as a mysterious science with deep and influential formulas found in
various aspects of life, not just Tantra Vidya itself.
• While its impact in history cannot be precisely proven, its mention in the Vedas
indicates its ancient origins.
• Tantra Vidya is still considered a mysterious and powerful Vidya.
• Tantra Vidya involves the use of Tratka (gazing), Mantra (chanting), and Yantra
(diagrams). Tratka involves staring without blinking at a point or object to focus the
mind and consciousness. Mantra chanting intensifies this effect, and Yantra is the
medium to activate the energy. The combination of these three elements is
considered very powerful for hypnotising someone.
• The sources mention the Shatkarma Vidhan (Six Actions) of Tantra as described in
the Agni Purana. These six Tantric actions are:
o Shanti Karma (pacification).
o Vashikaran (attraction/control).
o Stambhan (paralysis/stopping).
o Vidveshan (creating enmity).
o Uchchatan (driving away/causing displacement).

).
o Vashikaran (attraction/control).
o Stambhan (paralysis/stopping).
o Vidveshan (creating enmity).
o Uchchatan (driving away/causing displacement).
o Maran (causing death or death-like suffering).
• Besides the Shatkarma, the Agni Purana mentions Nine Experiments (Nau
Prayoga): Maran, Mohan, Stambhan, Vidveshan, Uchchatan, Vashikaran, Akarshan
(attraction), Yakshini Sadhana, and Rasayan Kriya (alchemy).
• These actions often require the Sadhana (spiritual practice) of various deities.
• Tantric Sadhana's original goal is साक्षात्कार (Siddhis - spiritual powers or
accomplishments) achieved through introspective practices.
• Tantric Sadhana is generally categorised into three paths: Vama Marga (Left Path),
Dakshina Marga (Right Path), and Madhyama Marga (Middle Path).
• Tantra is also claimed to be used for alleviating minor issues like the evil eye,
madness, planetary ill effects, bad days, instigated curses, mental agitation,
physical ailments, snake/scorpion bites, and boils.
2. Shiv Mohini Vidya (Washi Karan / Mohini Vidya)
• Shiv Mohini Vidya is a type of Tantra Vidya.
• It is a mysterious and powerful Sadhana mentioned in religious texts and Puranas.
• The Rigveda contains a

v Mohini Vidya is a type of Tantra Vidya.
• It is a mysterious and powerful Sadhana mentioned in religious texts and Puranas.
• The Rigveda contains a verse illustrating a Mohini application involving speech and
hands to heal and increase a person's strength.

• Mohini Vidya is also mentioned in the Kunjika Stotra of the Durga Saptashati, stating
that reciting it grants Siddhis in powerful Tantra Sadhanas like Maran, Mohan,
Vashikaran, Stambhan, and Uchchatan.
• Mohini Vidya is considered a powerful form of Tantra Mantra.
• It can affect a person on physical, mental, and spiritual levels.
• It is stated that Lord Shri Krishna was a skilled and powerful practitioner of Mohini
and Vashikaran Vidya, using it for various purposes and achieving impossible tasks.
• Purpose: To gain mental, emotional, or physical control over a person. It can
strengthen relationships.
• Techniques:
o Often uses Tratka (gazing at a point/object), Mantra (chanting), and Yantra
(diagrams).
o Specific Mantra: Om sham shivay mohini siddhe hreem om namah. This is
described as short but extremely powerful, showing effects quickly. It should
be chanted at least 21 times using a Rudraksha mala.
o Another Vashikaran Mantra mentioned is Om Vashikaranay Swaha. This is
considered excellent for Vashikaran Sadhana. It should be chanted 108 or
1000 times.
o Concentrating attention fully on the target person during chanting.
o Using a white cloth, a register/journal for Tantra

chanted 108 or
1000 times.
o Concentrating attention fully on the target person during chanting.
o Using a white cloth, a register/journal for Tantra Mantras, a lamp, incense,
sandalwood, Kalawa (sacred thread), flowers, and water as materials.
o Gazing into the flame of a lamp or a picture/name of the target person while
chanting. Visualizing the target person's mental state or nature being
influenced. Sending willpower towards the person.
• Timing: Can be performed on Mahashivratri or any sacred festival, or on a Monday
or Tuesday (though less effective). It is a one-day Sadhana, performable anytime
from sunrise to sunrise.
• Location: No specific restriction on location or clothing, but comfortable. Facing
North is more effective. Performing it in front of a Shivalinga in a Shiva temple is
essential for the Shiv Mohini Vidya mantra. Vashikaran Sadhana can be done in a
solitary or secret room.
• Preparation: Cleanliness is necessary; take a bath. Prepare materials. Mental
calmness and focus are needed.
• Benefits: Improvement in personal relationships, increased respect and love from
others. Success in love and stronger relationships. Good customer relations in
business. Freedom

sonal relationships, increased respect and love from
others. Success in love and stronger relationships. Good customer relations in
business. Freedom from court cases or legal issues. Reduced household conflicts
and increased peace. Can control a specific person by chanting with a resolve.
• Warnings/Precautions: Vashikaran Sadhana is powerful but its use and effect
depend on right intention and honesty. It is a responsible and deep Sadhana
requiring full information and guidance. Misuse can have negative effects on life.
Must only be used for positive purposes. Can affect mental and emotional
balance, so do carefully. Guru's guidance is essential and it should not be done
without it. It is a sensitive process that can be harmful if misused.

3. Kapal Sadhana
• Kapal Sadhana is described as a deep, mysterious, Ugra (fierce), and frightening
religious/Tantric practice.
• It involves a dead person's skull (Kapal), aiming to understand and use the energy,
powers, and secrets of the deceased. The skull is considered a centre of special power.
• Aghori and Avghad Sadhaks often follow this practice for success in fierce and terrifying
tasks. They use the Kapal as a vessel, believing it helps control ghosts, spirits, and their
own divine powers.
• It attempts to understand the cycle of life and death and connect with life's deeper
mysterious powers, often manifesting as strange, scary, and fierce Sadhanas.
• The more fear experienced during practice, the more powers the Sadhak gains. It is only
for those unafraid of intense forms of fear and Sadhana.
• Purpose/Benefits: Can gain great power, capable of overthrowing a small kingdom,
accessing hidden wealth/treasures, and even destroying cities.
• Requirement: Needs five skulls of warriors killed in battle. These must be collected
and used for the Sadhana. Never practice with fewer than five skulls, as it can disrupt the
life cycle and negatively impact the Sadhana.
• Location:

and used for the Sadhana. Never practice with fewer than five skulls, as it can disrupt the
life cycle and negatively impact the Sadhana.
• Location: Solitary, quiet, remote places like under a Banyan tree (Bat Vriksh), in a
cremation ground (Shmashan), or a burial ground (Marghat). These places are energy-
filled and suitable for nighttime practice. Under a Banyan tree is considered an ancient
and powerful location.
• Types:
o Using the head of a fresh corpse.
o Using a skull collected from a cremation ground.
• Duration: Can last from three to six months, or specifically 31 days for a particular
method mentioned. Patience and mental balance are essential.
• Materials: Five fresh heads of the deceased (for one method), or skulls from the
cremation ground. Lamp and incense (purify atmosphere, focus mind, attract powers).
Offerings of sweets, water, and alcohol (Madiira) for Kapaleshwar. Alcohol is part of
ancient customs for Aghori/Avghad साधक. Cow dung and soil (for creating a square
area). Buffalo hide (for sitting, considered sacred, provides stability). Blanket (for
warmth and focus). River water and flowers (for bathing/purifying skulls, symbols of
devotion).
• Method:
o For t

d, provides stability). Blanket (for
warmth and focus). River water and flowers (for bathing/purifying skulls, symbols of
devotion).
• Method:
o For the fresh head method: Bury five fresh heads in a square shape one hand
wide, one in the centre and one at each corner. Cover with soil and plaster
with cow dung. Sit on buffalo hide covered with a blanket on top and practice
for 31 days. Keep dry skulls in front and worship them.
o For the skull method: Set up the practice area. Choose a solitary place, like
under a Banyan tree. Prepare mentally in the evening. Gather necessary
items: lamp, incense, water, sweets, alcohol. Worship Kapaleshwar Shiva Ji
under the tree. The Sadhak sits on a black or white cloth, or naked with a
single cloth.
o Create a square shape on the ground with cow dung and soil, place the skulls
inside. Offer water and भोग (offerings) daily to the skulls.

o Specific ritual: Create a triangle yantra with Bhasma.
o Mantra: Om namo kapaleshwar kapal siddhi me kurute namah. This mantra
should be chanted.
o Mantra Jap: 5000 chants of a special mantra after the puja. This reveals faith
and prepares for deeper dimensions. This mantra must be chanted daily for
31 days.
• Process & Experiences: During the 31 days, the skulls may gradually begin to speak,
and the Sadhak receives divine knowledge from their conversation. This process can take
longer. It gets more intense over time, testing willpower and patience. Patience and faith
are most important; even if Siddhi is not quick, don't give up.
• After Sadhana: Must live a very restrained life and ensure no harm is done to anyone,
otherwise the Sadhana breaks and suffering increases.
• Warnings/Precautions: Extremely serious and dangerous. Should only be done under
the guidance of ancient texts and qualified Gurus. Can affect physical and mental
balance. We are not responsible for harmful or undisciplined behaviour. Proceed
with caution and prioritize your safety. Requires extreme focus, faith, and resolve. Do
not harm anyone after the Sadhana, as it will break and cause suffering.
4. Mahakal

itize your safety. Requires extreme focus, faith, and resolve. Do
not harm anyone after the Sadhana, as it will break and cause suffering.
4. Mahakal Bhasm Sadhana
• Mahakal Bhasm Sadhana is a mysterious and fierce Tantric Sadhana born from the
ashes of the cremation ground (Shmashan).
• It involves the use of Bhasma (ash), which is considered a medium through which
the Sadhak can control Kaal (time/death).
• It is a practice where the Sadhak sits before death and performs Sadhana.
• Shiva himself is adorned with Bhasma, signifying control over time and being the
lord after death. Bhasma is Shiva's attire, indicating that one who fears death cannot
be Shiva's Sadhak.
• Bhasma from the funeral pyre, when a body burns, is not just ash but a store of
energy, संस्कार (impressions/rites), and memory. It is pure and awakened Bhasma
capable of attracting Tantric power.
• Applying Bhasma as a Tilak (mark) on the forehead, heart, and navel awakens the
three energy centers. This increases the Sadhak's spiritual vibration and allows
feeling Tantra-related signals. Applying Bhasma as a Tilak means wearing death on
the body, starting the journey of conquering time.
• Purpose/Benefits: To gain c

ra-related signals. Applying Bhasma as a Tilak means wearing death on
the body, starting the journey of conquering time.
• Purpose/Benefits: To gain control over Kaal (time/death). To overcome the fear of
death. To gain power over time.
• Location: A solitary, frightening, but energy-filled location like a Shmashan, the
back of a Kali temple, or a secluded, deserted place. The Shmashan should be one
where a pyre has burned continuously for three nights, as that Bhasma is
considered awakened.
• Duration: A total of 31 days.
• Timing: Every night between 12:00 AM and 3:00 AM (Kaal Bela), which is considered
Mahakal's gaze where time stands still and Sadhana manifests.

• Preparation: Mind and body must be completely pure. Observe a Sattvic (pure) diet,
silence vow, and Brahmacharya (celibacy) for seven days prior. Stop speaking after
sunset. Collect Bhasma from the pyre of a warrior who died heroically (বীরগতি प्राप्त)
using one's own hands. This Bhasma is believed to contain energy that challenges
Kaal. Purify the Bhasma by keeping it in a copper vessel and washing with
Panchagavya (five products of cow) for three days. Then offer it at Mahakal's feet
and use it as a Tilak. This preparation involves burning fear and creating power from
ash.
• Materials: Bhasma from the pyre of a hero's body. Copper vessel. Black clothing or
black mat (accumulates energy, gives courage against fear). Rudraksha mala
(awakens Shiva consciousness). Kapal or skull vessel (used by experienced
Sadhaks, symbolises presence of death). Idol or picture of Bhootnath or Mahakal.
Incense, camphor, sandalwood (for purification and divinity). Trishul or Shiva's
symbol (protection and power, creates energy shield). Coconut, lemon tied with
black thread (for energy testing and absorbing negative effects, gives signals via
color/movement/position). Trikal Tilak of Kajal, Sindoor,

ied with
black thread (for energy testing and absorbing negative effects, gives signals via
color/movement/position). Trikal Tilak of Kajal, Sindoor, and Bhasma (prepares body
for mantra energy, activates mind and Ajna Chakra). Black sesame seeds (Kale Til).
Blue Aparajita flowers. 108 Japa seeds for Rudraksha mala. Kushasana (mat made
of Kush grass). Black sandalwood. Ghee lamp. Clay pot wrapped in red cloth for
water.
• Method:
o Select the location (Shmashan, Kali temple back, secluded spot).
o Light a South-facing lamp.
o Create a triangle with Bhasma.
o Apply Bhasma mixed with black sandalwood and water as a Tripund (three
horizontal lines) on the forehead, heart, and navel. This frees the Sadhak
from the fear of death.
o Mantra: Om hreem kal bhairvay mahakalay bhasmang roopay namah. This is
the core Tantric mantra.
o Mantra Jap: Chant the mantra 108 times daily using a Rudraksha mala.
o Meditation: While chanting, close eyes and meditate on the Shmashan fire
and Shiva's Tandava image.
o Offer black sesame seeds and Bhasma into the main triangle yantra.
o While offering काल ततलांजलल (oblation of time/death), say: Nam mrityave
swaha rakshaya namah.
o Final Night (31st day): Thi

he main triangle yantra.
o While offering काल ततलांजलल (oblation of time/death), say: Nam mrityave
swaha rakshaya namah.
o Final Night (31st day): This is the most powerful and risky night. Chant the
mantra all night.
o Specific mantra for final night: "Hey mrityu ke swami, mujhe mrityu se upar
utha do. Ab main samay se bandha nahi, main kaal ka bhi kaal hoon." (O Lord
of Death, raise me above death. Now I am not bound by time, I am the time of
time).
• Experiences during Sadhana:

o Days 1-7: Feeling of unknown fear, unseen force watching.
Strange/mysterious dreams (Shmashan, Bhasma, fire, Bhairav, skulls,
Tandava). Tingling in limbs, warmth on forehead.
o Days 8-21: Intense energy flow in the body, especially spine and Ajna Chakra.
Sharpness in eyes, depth in voice, mysterious aura on face. Sounds like
footsteps, low voices, lamp flickering - signs of energy presence.
o Days 22-31: Some Sadhaks experience internal dialogue with Bhairav or
Mahakal, receiving instructions. Mysterious Yantras, Mantras, glimpses of
past lives in dreams/meditation. Feeling of transcending time, where death
and life meet, experiencing Mahakal's Tandava energy.
• Signs of Siddhi (Achievement):
o Deep, mysterious, and definite signs indicate Siddhi is near or achieved.
These are internal experiences and cosmic signals.
o Bhasma may spontaneously form shapes (Trishul, eye, Rudraksha) in the
Sadhak's hand.
o A moment of feeling everything turn to शून्य (zero/void) - sounds, thoughts
stop, only darkness and energy. Entry into the door of Kaal.
o A very clear dream before Siddhi, seeing oneself in Shmashan before Bhairav
or in a divine form covered in Bhasma. Bhairav may appear and say

Kaal.
o A very clear dream before Siddhi, seeing oneself in Shmashan before Bhairav
or in a divine form covered in Bhasma. Bhairav may appear and say, "Now
you are ready, now you have the strength to clash with Kaal".
o Feeling a light rising from within, body feels light, head heavy, heart
transcends beats - sign of Siddhi energy activating.
o After the final day, the Sadhak's soul stabilises, fear disappears (of death,
obstacles). They see a power rising from the ashes awakening Mahakal
within.
• Warnings/Precautions: Requires immense energy, mental balance, restraint, and
dedication. Any carelessness, mockery, or ridicule can reverse the Sadhana's
effect.
5. Mahakali Sadhana
• Mahakali Sadhana is described as a deep and mysterious Tantric Sadhana.
• Mata Mahakali is the embodiment of the power of destruction and protection, and
the upliftment of the soul.
• Sadhana on the night of Amavasya (new moon), when Tamas (darkness) is
strongest, allows control over Tamasic powers.
• Mahakali Sadhana is not just a Tantric action but a deep spiritual process affecting
body, mind, and soul.
• Mahakali's form is a balance of destruction and protection. Her black colour
symbolises infinitude

spiritual process affecting
body, mind, and soul.
• Mahakali's form is a balance of destruction and protection. Her black colour
symbolises infinitude and cosmic power, beyond time and space. The garland of
skulls signifies destruction of ego, ignorance, and unrighteousness. Her four arms
with weapons (sword, trident) and a severed head symbolise readiness to destroy

unrighteousness and injustice. Lord Shiva calm beneath her feet indicates her
destructive power is for balance, not just विनाश (destruction).
• Purpose/Benefits: Gain willpower, divine power, and self-protection. Awakening
hidden power, destruction of fear, stability, and balance in life. Destruction of
enemies, removal of fear, freedom from illness, and spiritual balance. Divine power
on Amavasya makes the Sadhak invincible internally and externally. Experiences
both destructive and protective energy.
• Requirements/Qualifications: Sadhak must have special mental, physical, and
spiritual qualifications. Suitable only for Sadhaks who are mentally balanced and
spiritually mature. Without sufficient qualification, severe physical, mental, and
spiritual side effects can occur. Mental balance, spiritual maturity, and Guru's
initiation are essential. Body, mind, and soul must be pure and balanced. Must be
able to handle intense energies. Fear, doubt, instability, or physical weakness make
the Sadhana potentially harmful. Weak willpower cannot bear the experiences.
• Brahmacharya (Celibacy): Strict observance of Brahmacharya (in thought, speech,
action) is essential. This preserves energ

not bear the experiences.
• Brahmacharya (Celibacy): Strict observance of Brahmacharya (in thought, speech,
action) is essential. This preserves energy, strengthens mental and spiritual power,
keeps the mind calm and focused, and helps Kundalini Shakti awaken.
• Diet: Sattvic (pure) and pure diet is necessary for pure and positive effect. Sattvic
food (fruits, milk, honey, मूंग की दाल - moong dal, dry fruits, Gangajal, desi ghee)
calms Tamasic and Rajasic energies. Avoid meat, alcohol, garlic, onion,
fried/spicy/Tamasic food. Sattvic diet stabilises mind and body. Avoiding prohibited
foods prevents the Sadhana's energy from being disrupted and protects
mental/spiritual strength.
• Purity: Mental and physical purity is essential. Bathe before Sadhana. Sit on a clean
mat. Keep mind calm and stable, avoid external noise.
• Mental State: Must be mentally stable. Completely abandon fear, illusion, and
doubt during Sadhana. Willpower and fearlessness must be present. Concentration
and focus are necessary. Weak willpower makes the Sadhana ineffective. Fear and
doubt make the energy harmful.
• Guru's Guidance: Initiation by a Guru is essential. Guru provides secret methods,
mantras, Tantra

effective. Fear and
doubt make the energy harmful.
• Guru's Guidance: Initiation by a Guru is essential. Guru provides secret methods,
mantras, Tantra knowledge. Guru's guidance stabilises the Sadhak mentally and
spiritually. Guru's blessings bring quicker results. Controlling Mahakali's power
requires Guru's grace.
• Who Should Avoid: Individuals with weak mental strength, instability, physical
weakness, fear, or doubt. Pregnant women should avoid it as their bodies cannot
withstand the intense energy, and the imaginative energy can harm the fetus.
• Materials: Selection is crucial for success, varying by Dakshina Marga or Vama
Marga. Dakshina Marga uses Sattvic/simple materials. Vama Marga uses special
materials to awaken Tamasic/Ugra energy. Materials must be purified and
consecrated.
o Red and Black Clothing: Black for absorbing Tamasic energy and negative
powers. Red for power, destruction, energy. Helps awaken Mahakali's fierce
energy.

o Mala: Kali Hakik mala (awakens Ugra/Tamasic energy) and Rudraksha mala
(establishes energy balance, Shiva-Shakti balance).
o Other Materials: Bhasma (symbol of destructive energy), Kajal (protects
from Tamasic energy, sharpens sight), Rakta Chandan (red sandalwood,
awakens gentle/protective energy). Mustard oil lamp (destroys Tamasic
energy, purifies location, quickly attracts grace). Lemon (destroys negative
energy, helpful in removing Tamasic powers, used in Havan/sacrifice).
Alcohol (offered as भोग/offering, part of Vama Marga, awakens Tamasic
energy). Meat (used in Ugra Tantric Sadhana, part of Vama Marga, brings
quick Siddhi).
o Specific method materials: Rakta Chandan, Bhasma, black sesame seeds,
108 red flowers. A clay lamp with mustard oil and clove. Ash of a corpse (if
available).
• Method:
o Timing: Best time is the second part of the night (12 AM - 3 AM) on Amavasya.
This is when negative/Tamasic energies are active and Mahakali's destructive
influence is strong. Also possible during specific times on Holi, Mahalaya
Pitru Paksha अष्टमी (8th day) or चतदु दशी (14th day).
o Location: Shmashan bhumi (Kali's destructive aspect is active), under a
Peepal tree (controls Tamas

Paksha अष्टमी (8th day) or चतदु दशी (14th day).
o Location: Shmashan bhumi (Kali's destructive aspect is active), under a
Peepal tree (controls Tamasic energy, awakens positive energy), secluded
place (avoids external disturbance), secret place (public practice destroys
effect). Specific location mentioned as where a pyre has recently cooled.
o Preparation: Bathe before Sadhana, purify body and mind (use Gangajal or
holy water). Wear red or black clothes. Sit on a red mat or Kushasana, facing
North. Sprinkle Gangajal or Gomutra (cow urine) to purify the location. Apply
Tilak of Bhasma and Rakta Chandan. Light a mustard oil lamp in front of
Mahakali. For Vedic method, chant purification mantra (Om apavitrah
pavitro...) 11 times.
o Yantra/Mandala: Create a triangular Yantra with Rakta Chandan, Bhasma,
and Kajal at the Sadhana spot. Light mustard oil lamps at the four corners of
the Yantra. Place Mahakali's idol or picture in the center. A triangular Yantra is
a symbol of Tantric energy.
o Invocation (Aahvaan): Close eyes, breathe deeply. Chant the invocation
mantra: Om Kreem Kalika Namah. Visualise Mahakali's black form, garland
of skulls, blood-stained tongue, weapons, fierce eyes,

y. Chant the invocation
mantra: Om Kreem Kalika Namah. Visualise Mahakali's black form, garland
of skulls, blood-stained tongue, weapons, fierce eyes, destructive form.
Focus completely on her form. Invocation activates her energy within.
o Mantra Jap: Use Rudraksha or Kali Hakik mala. Chant: Om Kreem Kreem
Kreem Mahakalika Phat Swaha, or Om Kreem Kalika Namah, or Om Aim
Hreem Kleem Kalika Namah. Chant 108 or 1008 times. Visualise Mahakali's
form (third eye, sword, fierce form) while chanting. Mantra Jap is key to
success, flowing her energy within.
o Meditation (Dhyana): Sit on Kushasana with eyes closed. Meditate on
Mahakali's form (four arms with weapons, Abhaya mudra, black clothes,

skull garland, blood-stained face, fierce eyes). Visualize her energy entering
your body. Maintain a stable and focused mind.
o Offerings (Bhog): Offer lamp, lemon, meat, and alcohol after mantra jap.
Offer flowers and Bhasma at Mahakali's feet after Sadhana completion.
o Havan (Fire Ritual): Increases the effect of Sadhana. Use wood and
camphor in the Havan Kund. Light fire with mustard oil. Chant: Om Kreem
Kalika Swaha with each offering. Offerings of lemon, meat, and alcohol. Ask
for blessings after Havan.
o Specific method ritual: Create a triangular yantra with Bhasma. Place lamp in
the center. Offer flowers on the yantra. Chant mantra 108 times.
o Specific mantra for Shitla Chandika: Om Hreem Kleem Shitalay Chandikay
Swaha. Used by specific Sadhaks.
o Ending: After mantra jap, ask forgiveness for a sick or suffering soul. This is a
secret for receiving Shitla Chandika's special grace.
• Results & Signs of Siddhi: Effects felt immediately. Increased willpower, courage,
mental stability. Destruction of negative energies, defeat of enemies. Experience of
divinity in mind and soul. Specific signs confirm Sadhana completion and grace.
o Immense power in the mind, feeling of sudden

enemies. Experience of
divinity in mind and soul. Specific signs confirm Sadhana completion and grace.
o Immense power in the mind, feeling of sudden willpower, mental power. Fear
vanishes, ability to face any situation arises. Mahakali's destructive energy
flows within. Increase in courage, stability, confidence.
o Seeing Mahakali in dreams (fierce or calm form). Receiving guidance or
messages in dreams. Seeing fire, corpses, or blood in dreams (signs of
Siddhi). Grace is established, receiving inspiration to move forward.
o Automatic destruction or change in behaviour of enemies. Enemies feel fear.
Opponents are naturally defeated. Enemy behaviour becomes calm and
supportive. Kali's destructive power controls enemies.
o Sudden freedom from fear and mental tension. Mental peace, complete
fearlessness, and confidence without external reason. Flow of spiritual
energy. Mahakali's power is stable within, Sadhak is protected mentally and
spiritually.
o Body trembling or energy flow during or after mantra jap. Feeling energy flow
in various chakras. Slight pressure or warmth around the forehead and heart.
Mahakali's power has permanently entered the Sadhak. Muladhara, Ajna,
and Sahasra

us chakras. Slight pressure or warmth around the forehead and heart.
Mahakali's power has permanently entered the Sadhak. Muladhara, Ajna,
and Sahasrara chakras become active.
• Warnings/Potential Dangers: Extremely powerful and fierce. Involves destructive
and Tamasic energy. Serious negative consequences if not done with purity and
rules. Take extreme caution.
o Never do it as a joke or for entertainment/power display. Without faith and
dedication, adverse effects occur. Taking Mahakali's power lightly can be
fatal. Do with full surrender and faith.
o Immediately stop if feeling uncomfortable, fearful, or mentally
disturbed. Consult Guru before trying again. Fear indicates obstacle. Only
do with mental stability and fearlessness.

o Mantra pronunciation must be clear and pure. Wrong pronunciation leads to
failure, creates negative energy. Keep mind stable during chanting. Pure
chanting activates power, impure leads to adverse results.
o Observe silence during Sadhana, don't talk to anyone. External conversation
disrupts energy flow. Keep mobile/devices away. Maintain full concentration.
External disturbance affects energy.
o Keep the room door closed. Prevents external interference. No other person
should be in the room. Closed door regulates energy flow.
o If Sadhana is unsuccessful, consult Guru before trying again. Do not try
again by oneself. Performing Ugra Sadhana without Guru guidance can have
adverse results. Guru guidance is mandatory.
6. Maran Tantra (Including Putli Maran Tantra)
• Maran Tantra is one of the six Shatkarma Vidhan in Tantra, meaning causing death or
death-like suffering. It is considered a forbidden act (Nishiddh Karma).
• Described in the Agni Purana.
• It involves increasing the air element to destroy memory, causing buzzing in ears,
pain/disorders in veins/bones. Increasing the Pitta element to disrupt digestion or
cause boils, gangrene, cancer. Increasing the Kapha element to ca

pain/disorders in veins/bones. Increasing the Pitta element to disrupt digestion or

• Maran is used for enemy destruction.
• Types of Maran:
o Immediate death: Such experiments are known as Ghaat or Kritya.
Described as a powerful Tantric fire weapon that is invisible but hits the
target like a bullet.
o Slow death: Known as Mand Maran. Can make a person ill with fever,
diarrhea, pain, paralysis, madness, delusion, like spreading epidemics with
germ bombs.
• These practices are considered possible. Just as poison or weapons can kill,
invisible means can also trigger nature's destructive atoms to attack a target.
• Also mentions the concept of "Mrityu Kiran" (death rays) as a modern scientific
invention analogous to these effects, causing internal damage leading to death or
chronic illness.
• Ancient knowledge of giving curses is mentioned as similar, where concentrated
spiritual powers were directed to cause harm.
• Tantric Abichars (malicious practices) can also harm others similarly.

ous practices) can also harm others similarly.
• Performing Maran, Uchchatan Abichars expends significant energy of the

• Maran Vidya Sadhana Method:
o This is described as a dangerous Vidya.
o One type is Putli Vidya (Doll Vidya), which is similar to practices in Africa
(Voodoo).

o Putli Vidya is not just destructive; it can also treat severe incurable diseases
from a distance, and is used for Vashikaran, Mohan, Stambhan, Uchchatan,
and Maran.
o Process:
▪ Knowledge of the enemy's (target's) Nakshatra (lunar mansion).
▪ Collecting materials based on the target's Nakshatra tree, hair, or
lower garment. Materials include black mud, ash from a pyre or the
target's object, rock salt, dried ginger, black pepper, kitchen soot,
garlic, asafoetida, geru (ochre), yellow mustard, black mustard,
sugarcane juice/jaggery, neem bark paste. Selection of materials
depends on the desired action.
▪ Making a doll (Putli) from Urad flour.
▪ Inserting the target's hair and body hair into the doll.
▪ The doll's length and parts correspond to specific divisions (27
Nakshatras x 4 divisions = 108 types).
▪ Using astrological positions (8th house for malefic acts, 5th for
auspicious) and matching the time, date, and sign.
▪ Knowledge of Amrit Sthan (Nectar locations) and Vish Sthan (Poison
locations) in the body, which change daily based on Lunar ततथि (date)
and differ between male and female bodies. The timing of the action
is chosen based on these locations.
▪ Prana Pratishtha: I

sed on Lunar ततथि (date)
and differ between male and female bodies. The timing of the action
is chosen based on these locations.
▪ Prana Pratishtha: Infusing life into the doll, a separate process.
▪ Performing ritualistic actions and Havan after Prana Pratishtha.
▪ Actions like piercing, cutting, heating, or putting the doll in liquid
affect the target person wherever they are.
• Mechanism (according to sources): When a practitioner uses Maran Tantra, they
must find a Jiva (life force) wandering in the universe from someone who died an
unnatural death. This Jiva is directed towards the target person using mantras. The
target will then die the same way the original person died (e.g., vomiting blood,
hanging).
• Hair, teeth, etc. separated from the body remain active for 90 days, producing
energy positive to the person's body energy, with the person's body as the receiver.
Actions on the doll (piercing, cutting, heating, immersion) affect the target.
• Symptoms of Maran Tantra Attack: Increased heartbeat, increased breathing rate
(resistance from the body). More severe attack means faster pulse. Intense pain like
needles or spears, cutting/tearing pain, fever, madness. The victim su

stance from the body). More severe attack means faster pulse. Intense pain like
needles or spears, cutting/tearing pain, fever, madness. The victim suffers as long
as the doll is being tortured.
• Countering Maran Tantra: A skilled Tantric can stop these practices, make them
ineffective, or even reverse the attack onto the practitioner. If a Ghaat, Kritya, or
Chowki (specific types of attacks) is reversed by another Tantric, misfortune falls
upon the initiator.
• If someone suspects they are affected, they should immediately contact a qualified
Tantric.

• One difficult way to stop it is to find and destroy the doll.
• Another effective way is to change the victim's entire energy equation so they
become negative to the doll's waves, causing the waves to keep searching for their
target. The sources surprisingly relate this to the scientific principles behind
missiles, mobile phones, radio signals, and sensors. Tantra is not magic but works
on scientific principles.
• Par Vidya Bhakshani Experiment: This is a Tantric practice to neutralise other
Vidya, specifically aimed at consuming (destroying) opposing Mantras, Tantras, and
Yantras used by opponents. It is called "Brahma-astra Roopini Par Vidya Grasini". It
is linked to Mother Baglamukhi.
o Purpose: To dissolve and consume the opponent's Vidya (Mantras, Tantras,
Yantras). Can also destroy गण (Gana - possibly referring to negative entities
or effects).
o Mantra: A 127-letter mantra is given. It includes parts like "Om Hreem

Om Acht Par Pragyaharani Pragya Bhramshay Bhramshay Om Acht
Stambhanastra Roopini Buddhim Nashaya Nashaya Pdi Gyan Bhaksha
Bh

sini Bhakshaya Bhakshaya
Om Acht Par Pragyaharani Pragya Bhramshay Bhramshay Om Acht
Stambhanastra Roopini Buddhim Nashaya Nashaya Pdi Gyan Bhaksha

o Siddhi: Achieved by 100,000 chants, completed in 14-21 days.
o Effect: If done correctly, no Vidya will work within a 5 km radius. Chanting

o Method: Requires a specific resolve (Sankalpa) stating the purpose. Includes

o Requirement: Must receive initiation from a Guru (Dakshinaachar or

Guru.
o Warning: Do not perform any action without the supervision of an

uru.

7. Mrit Sanjeevani Sadhana
• Mrit Sanjeevani Kalpa Sadhana is a secret, powerful, and rare Vidya
(knowledge/science) that transcends the boundaries between life and death.

• It is a mysterious journey that takes the soul beyond death. Not just a mantra,
medicine, or story, but the universe's most secret sound that can revive the dead.
Only heard by those whose hearts don't fear death but dare to master it. Described
as the rebirth of the soul.
• It is a Vidya beyond disease and death. It can not only reawaken life but also regain
the energy lost due to distortion, disease, and death.
• It teaches that disease and death are not just physical events but spiritual truths
that can be changed by inner powers. By directing energy correctly, not just the body
but the soul can be given strength and life.
• Mentioned as originating from the secret branches of the Atharvaveda. These
branches are silent, in the stillness of Shrutis, where there is energy vibration, not
just mantra sound.
• It is based on a combination of three main Tantric streams:
o Bhairavi Tantra: Where death at the peak of power is transformed into
liberation (Moksha).
o Garuda Tantra: Famous for alleviating disease and poison, a storehouse of
special methods for life protection.
o Kaumara Tantra: A rare path that converts the pure, unstained energy of
childhood into life force.
o The sourc

special methods for life protection.
o Kaumara Tantra: A rare path that converts the pure, unstained energy of
childhood into life force.
o The sources state that this Sadhana born from the mixture of these three is a
deep bridge between life and death.
• It is a high-level Tantric process and an extraordinary experience connecting the
Sadhak to the power beyond life and death.
• Its knowledge is kept secret and is highly sacred. It is protected silently through
Guru tradition for centuries. Only Yogis, Tantrics, and Saints who live their lives as a
Sadhana have achieved its highest knowledge. Taught secretly within the Guru-
disciple tradition. Its misuse can lead to spiritual destruction.
• Only reaches those who have performed complete Sadhana, who are strong
physically and mentally, and have stepped onto the path of spiritual awakening.
Limited to special and responsible individuals.
• Purity: Purity of the Sadhak is crucial for operating this Vidya. Only one with a
sacred purpose focused on self-welfare can follow this path. Using it only for
personal gain can lead to dreadful and destructive results. Spiritual maturity and a
pure purpose are the main pillars.
• Mrit Sanjeeva

ng it only for
personal gain can lead to dreadful and destructive results. Spiritual maturity and a
pure purpose are the main pillars.
• Mrit Sanjeevani meaning: 'Mrit' means death or state of death, body/soul losing
energy. 'Sanjeevani' means divine energy source that can revive the dead or sick. It
is an extraordinary power connecting the soul to physical/mental existence, filling
the body with Prana Shakti. It is also the power to revive spiritual life.
• Key Components:
o Sanjeevani Beej Mantra: Not an ordinary Beej Mantra, but a sound cycle
connecting the Sadhak's inner life energy to divinity. Not written in texts,
passes from Guru's heart to disciple's soul. Chanting takes consciousness to
a level where it can command death. It is not a plea. Sidha (achieved) during
the third quarter of the night on a specific Yantra and posture in a state

deeper than silence. A specific, potent Beej Mantra formula is given in a
confidential code style for those stable in Tantra and Dhyana: Shree Aikleem
Sauh Yonaye Swaha. This mantra is not for public chanting; only mental,
silent recitation in deep Samadhi state. It is an energy sound, not just words.
▪ Shree: Pure vibration of consciousness, awakens Parashakti.
▪ Aim: Sound of Brahma Vidya, gives awareness to energy.
▪ Kleem: Kama Beej, but here signifies Rasa (essence) beyond Kama.
▪ Sauh: Shiva's silent element, takes the seed to Sahasrara.
▪ Yonaye: Invokes the cosmic door.
▪ Swaha: Symbol of self-surrender and merging.
▪ Usage: Mental chanting only, in deep meditation, 108 times nightly.
Focus on the triangular Yantra. Body fully silent, mind in Shiv Bhava.
▪ Warning: Viewing this Sadhana carnally can lead to mental distortion,
madness, or impotence. Guru initiation, secluded place, complete
Brahmacharya, and night Sadhana are necessary.
o Sanjeevani Kalpa Aushadhi (Herbal Medicine): Without Kalpa, mantra is
incomplete; without Kalpa, power doesn't manifest in gross form. Uses rare
herbs found only in specific seasons/locations (near Shmashans, in
mountain caves, active only o

alpa, power doesn't manifest in gross form. Uses rare
herbs found only in specific seasons/locations (near Shmashans, in
mountain caves, active only on lunar eclipse nights). Used not just for
consumption but consecrated and worn, or offered as smoke oblation to
activate the subtle body. Mixed with consecrated water to raise
consciousness. Gives Sanjeevani touch to soul, not just body.
o Yam Bhairav Dhyana (Meditation): The most courageous and decisive step.
Meditate on the form commonly called death, but which the Sadhak sees as
the threshold of transition. Yam Bhairav is the lord of death, protector of the
door of rebirth, not destruction. Involves invoking the third eye on a skull,
offering red flowers to Bhairav's Pindi (symbolic form), and meditating on
Yam Bhairav's form in a specific Tantric posture until the heart rate slows.
This teaches controlling the fearful energy of death, transforming that control
into Sanjeevani.
• Sadhana Method:
o Timing: Performed at midnight on Purnima (full moon) or Amavasya (new
moon). This is when lunar and Tamasic energies are at their peak and subtle
realms are open. The start is at this junction when life and death blur.
o Duration: A 21-d

en lunar and Tamasic energies are at their peak and subtle
realms are open. The start is at this junction when life and death blur.
o Duration: A 21-day intense Sadhana. Sadhak must not only chant but also
purify oneself. Must be completed in 21 days.
o Diet: Only Sattvic food without garlic or onion and specifically Tantrically
approved diet (no salt or spices). Aims to purify the body and reveal the soul.
o Mantra Jap: At night, in darkness and silence. Sit on a specific Yantra (drawn
on Bhoj Patra or made from purified clay on clean ground). The secret Beej
Mantra "Mrit Sanjeevani Mahashakti Namah" is chanted. This mantra pulls
the Sadhak's consciousness out of the body and connects it to the energy
beyond death. Chant 108 times daily with specific Pranayama (breath

control) and Mudra (hand gestures). Done in a secluded place free from
external energy interference.
o Tantric Havan & Oblations (Aahuti): Three special stages (5th, 11th, 17th
days) called transition days. Specific oblations (dried herbs, red flowers,
ghee, special life Sanjeevani powder) are offered. Chant "Yakshini Mrit
Sanjeevani Swaha" with oblations. Oblations influence subtle realms and
ensure Sanjeevani energy enters Yama's gate.
o Completion: On the 21st day, when the mantra's echo transcends the inner
mind and the Sadhak builds a relationship with death instead of fearing it,
the Mrit Sanjeevani Sadhana's door opens. The Sadhak, previously just a
body, becomes a representative of the deity standing between life and death.
• Benefits (Beyond reviving the dead):
o Provides an invisible protective shield around the Sadhak with regular
practice. Protects from energy attacks and ghost/spirit obstacles.
8. Shitla Chandika Shmashan Sadhana
• Shitla Chandika Shmashan Sadhana is a Tantric mystery.
• Shitla Chandika is known in popular belief as the goddess who cures diseases and
prevents smallpox. However, in Tantric texts, she appears in a different, fierce, and
unseen form.
• Th

r belief as the goddess who cures diseases and
prevents smallpox. However, in Tantric texts, she appears in a different, fierce, and
unseen form.
• This Goddess resides in the ashes of the Shmashan, among dead bodies, where life
and death meet. Her name opens only to Sadhaks who see disease not just as a
physical problem but an energy battle.
• Mentioned in texts like Guli Tantra, Shitalopanishad, and secret Tantric branches of
the Atharvaveda.
• She is seen not just as a destroyer of epidemics but can reverse death, pull a
drowning soul back from the threshold from which no one returns, if the Sadhak
knows how to call her.
• The Sadhana is for those connected to the Tantric tradition who understand Tantric
healing not just through herbs but energy elements, ghostly powers, and subtle
causes of disease. They "read" disease like a prophecy.
• Her power is so immense that fearful spirits and black magic cannot harm the
Sadhak.
• Purpose/Benefits: Not just relief from diseases, but overcoming energy blockages
and spirit obstacles behind diseases. Understanding and controlling fierce
messages of death. Divine vision of disease (knowing a disease before it comes).
Healing the seemingly

diseases. Understanding and controlling fierce
messages of death. Divine vision of disease (knowing a disease before it comes).
Healing the seemingly dead (extending the limit of life). Power to control spirit
obstacles (giving peace to souls causing disease). Protection from capital
punishment. An invisible shield against premature death. Controlling diseases
caused by ghosts and spirits that doctors/medicine cannot cure. Provides strength
and power. Destruction of enemies, removal of fear, self-protection, mental
balance. Quick grace from Mahakali (or implied Shitla Chandika) and removal of all
life obstacles.

• Requirements: Only possible by sitting on knees in the Shmashan mud and
invoking her form. Requires connection to Tantric tradition. Sadhana is a war fought
within against one's own fear, disease, and death. Requires concentration,
dedication, and patience for success.
• Form of Shitla Chandika: Extremely fierce, blood-coloured, terrifying, adorned with
Bhasma. Garland of dead skulls on her head. Hands hold a sword, trident, and
flame, not a broom. Vehicle is a blood-soaked Shmashan Bhairav, not a donkey.
Eyes like fire, blood drops on tongue, body covered in Shmashan ash. She controls
burning diseases (दाह), illness (रोग), death (मरण), and curses (शाप).
• Sadhana Method:
o Timing: Not just any day/place, bound by time and place. Special nights are
chosen: Gupt Amavasya (when realms of gods and spirits intermingle), Holi
night (when fierce form appears from fire), Mahalaya Pitru Paksha's 8th or
14th day (when ancestors' souls are close and the veil of death is thinnest).
The first quarter of the night is chosen.
o Location: Where the hot ash of a pyre has just cooled, where the memory of
dead bodies still lingers in the air. An active Shmashan where death has
recently occurred. I

e hot ash of a pyre has just cooled, where the memory of
dead bodies still lingers in the air. An active Shmashan where death has
recently occurred. If ash is not available, sit under a Peepal tree.
o Posture: Sit facing East, the door of new consciousness and life, where the
goddess reverses death. Sit in a triangle on Bhasma.
o Action: Sadhak sits silently, applies Bhasma on the Kapal (skull/forehead).
Only ash, silence, and a distant dog's howl. Then speaks slowly: "Hey
Chandika, I am here, made from ash and ready to invite death".
o Materials: Special Yantra of Shitla Chandika described in secret Tantras.
Rakta Chandan, Bhasma, black sesame seeds, 108 red flowers. A clay lamp
with mustard oil and clove. Ash of a corpse (if available). If not available,
Sadhana under a Peepal tree.
o Mantra: Om Hreem Kleem Shitalay Chandikay Swaha. Used by specific
Sadhaks. Chant with attention.
o Ritual: Create a triangular Yantra with Bhasma. Place the lamp in the center
of the triangle. Offer flowers on the Yantra. Chant the mantra 108 times.
o Ending: At the end, ask forgiveness for a sick or suffering soul. This is the
secret to receiving Shitla Chandika's special grace.
o General Mahakali

Ending: At the end, ask forgiveness for a sick or suffering soul. This is the
secret to receiving Shitla Chandika's special grace.
o General Mahakali Sadhana method steps (likely applicable principles):
Bathing, purifying location, applying Tilak, lighting lamp, invocation, mantra
chanting, meditation, offerings, Havan.
o Specific Mantra Jap: 108 times daily. Prior and after, recite सप्तश्लोकी दगु ाद
(Saptashloki Durga).
• Warnings: It is an Ugra (fierce) and intense goddess. It is one of the most dangerous
Sadhanas. Without the supervision of an experienced Guru, do not perform any
practice.
9. Parashakti Yoni Sadhana

• Parashakti Yoni Sadhana is described as an extremely fierce and powerful Sadhana.
• It is called a high-level Tantric process.
• Parashakti is not a goddess or a form, but the fundamental consciousness of the
entire creation, from which Brahma's will, Vishnu's order, and Shiva's destruction
are born.
• She is not male or female, not sound or silence; she is the Parayoni element from
which the first vibration of creation emerged.
• The Parashakti Yoni is a mystery; Vedas became silent, Rishis became still in
Samadhi when she first appeared.
• "Yoni Rupini Parashakti" is not a Vedic mantra but the birth sound of the cosmos.
Yoni here means the ancient door where energy first held the seed of
consciousness, not just a body part.
• The Parashakti Yoni door is invisible, not in temples or Tantra diagrams, but appears
where the Sadhak has gone beyond Kama (desire), beyond the male-female
distinction, dissolved in the vibration of energy.
• It is not an imagination but the living vibration of the cosmos, appearing only in the
inner consciousness of a Sadhak in Shiv Bhava (state of Shiva).
• This Sadhana is so fierce and powerful that not every Sadhak can do it. Not for those
who only wa

usness of a Sadhak in Shiv Bhava (state of Shiva).
• This Sadhana is so fierce and powerful that not every Sadhak can do it. Not for those
who only want power, but for those who dare to dissolve themselves. For those who
have made Shiv Bhava their form, whose minds are naked of desire, attachment,
and ego, who have awakened the pure energy flow from Muladhara to Sahasrara,
and for whom male or female is not a subject but only one element, Parashakti.
• Requirements/Qualifications: Only for complete Brahmacharis (celibates),
initiated in Tantra, and full of Shiv Bhava. Otherwise, practice can cause mental
deterioration, sexual perversion, or energetic upheaval.
• Discipline: Tantra discipline is harder than iron. Sadhak must observe
Brahmacharya not just physically, but also in thoughts, sight, and even desire.
• Duration: A 31-day fire test or a 108-night Yoni journey Sadhana. This is not just a
period but a journey, a fire sacrifice where oblation is offered daily.
• Method:
o Daily practice in silence. No mantras are spoken, no imagination is used,
only solitary Shiv Bhava should exist.
o Timing: Night Sadhana.
o Preparation: Sit alone, naked or in white clothes, on a black mat.

ination is used,
only solitary Shiv Bhava should exist.
o Timing: Night Sadhana.
o Preparation: Sit alone, naked or in white clothes, on a black mat.
o Materials: White cloth (or complete nudity with one cloth). Black mat
(symbol of the zero/void where Parashakti appears). Triangular Yantra (Yoni
Mudra shape, encompasses the three cosmic vibrations: will, knowledge,
action). Created with red or white sandalwood, it becomes the door to the
Sadhak's consciousness. Only one lamp is needed (not four, not a series).
The lamp burns in front of the navel, the center where energy rises and the
body is consumed. Parashakti Beej Mantra. Not in texts/scriptures,
descends from the Guru's silence. Controlled mental and spiritual chanting.
These are not just materials but keys to the cosmic Yoni door.

o Mantra Jap: The Beej Mantra is an energy signal, not a publicly chanted word.
It is received from the Guru in silence, chanted through breath and
consciousness vibration beyond words.
o Initial Stage (Silent Samadhi): Silence not just externally, but within. Sit
alone, eyes closed. An image appears inside, below the navel – a triangular
Yantra. This Yantra slowly lights up, and a red vibration appears in its center –
the Parashakti Beej Mantra. Only mental chanting.
o Energy Entry (Spinal Ignition): A line rises from the triangle in front of the
Sadhak, not burning but consuming every nerve. Sadhak feels intense energy
rising from the triangular Yantra into the spine. This vibration rises from
Muladhara, awakening every Chakra, reaching Sahasrara (crown of the
head). Upon reaching Sahasrara, a silent explosion occurs, and the Sadhak's
identity/ego completely dissolves.
o Vision of Parashakti (Without Form): After the Sadhana is complete, the
Sadhak does not see a deity, but only a pulsating energy womb. No form,
color, or idol is seen, only a vast, pulsating energy womb with light, rhythm,
and silence. This is the Parayoni element from which Brahma, Vishnu, and
Shiva emerged as m

en, only a vast, pulsating energy womb with light, rhythm,
and silence. This is the Parayoni element from which Brahma, Vishnu, and
Shiva emerged as mere thoughts.
• Results: Body vibrations, tears from eyes, fire-like waves in the heart, and a
compassion that erases all distinctions. Sadhak no longer sees male or female, only
energy flowing in every form. Touching causes body vibrations as if the body itself
has become the vibration of Parashakti Yoni. Puja is no longer needed, as the
Sadhak is flowing with Parashakti, not connected to a deity.
• Warnings/Precautions: As mentioned, only for qualified Sadhaks, improper
practice leads to severe negative consequences. Viewing it carnally results in
mental distortion, madness, or impotence. Requires Guru initiation, secluded
place, complete Brahmacharya, and night Sadhana.
10. Vanaspati Tantra (Tantric Techniques for Talking to Trees)
• Vanaspati Tantra is the art of listening to the murmuring of the Earth's
consciousness when trees speak.
• When humans speak there is noise, but when trees speak, only silence murmurs.
• The art of greeting trees (Vriksha Namaste) is also a form of Tantra.
• It originates from the union of Shaiva, Shak

es speak, only silence murmurs.
• The art of greeting trees (Vriksha Namaste) is also a form of Tantra.
• It originates from the union of Shaiva, Shakta, and Aghor traditions. Trees are seen
not just as nature but as ancient pillars of consciousness.
• Classical references are found in Rigveda (Vanaspati as forest healers), Atharvaveda
(disease-curing mantras for specific trees), Kulav Tantra (mention of forest deity
Sadhana), and Skanda Purana (description of conscious trees).
• Specific trees are associated with different deities and purposes: Peepal (Shiva,
Moksha, meditation), Neem (Devi Durga, disease peace, aura protection), Bat
(Banyan) (Guru, ancestors, ancestor contact, knowledge), Palash (Bhairavi, fire,
transformation), Rudraksha (Shiva, Shiva Bhairav, mantra Siddhi, inner awakening).

• Every plant contains an element that can be awakened by the correct mantra and
rules.
• Purpose: To communicate with trees. To deepen spiritual practice and gain divine
connection. To bring peace and understanding.
• Techniques:
o Tree Selection (Vriksha Chayan): Choose a tree based on a dream or state
of mind. Calm the mind before sleeping, let go of daily experiences. In
dreams or mind, feel a tree most suitable for you. When meeting the tree, feel
connected to it.
o Intention Setting & Purification (Sankalpa aur Shuddhi): Prepare mind for
cleansing and purification. Morning (Brahmamuhurta) is best for natural
peace and energy. Bathe, wear white clothes, and make a resolve: "O
Vanaspati Deva, I wish to hear your voice".
o Touch & Vibration Listening (Sparsh aur Kampan Sunna): Place hand on
the tree trunk and listen for its vibration. Close eyes, try to connect. Feel a
vibration or energy transferring from the tree.
o Mantra Jap: Chant Om Vanaspate Namah 108 times. If a specific tree is
chosen, chant its specific mantra. Examples: Neem (Om Hreem Kalyanyai
Namah), Peepal (Om Namah Shivaya), Bat (Om Guruve Namah).
o Meditation (Dhyana): Meditate daily under the tree for at least 3

Neem (Om Hreem Kalyanyai
Namah), Peepal (Om Namah Shivaya), Bat (Om Guruve Namah).
o Meditation (Dhyana): Meditate daily under the tree for at least 30 minutes.
Keep mind calm, feel vibrations. You might get thoughts or feelings, or
experience peace. Helps build a deep connection and establish
communication.
o Offering (Pushpanjali): Give devotion and respect to the tree. Offer milk,
turmeric, fruit, or water at the roots. Symbol of inner surrender and spiritual
offering. Energy exchange deepens the connection.
o Tuning into the Tree's Energy (Tree ki Urja se Samanjasya): Understand the
tree's consciousness and feel its effect on your body. When near, try to
match your inner vibration with the tree's. Feel the energy from the tree
throughout your body. Try to merge your life force with the tree's, like feeling
warmth in hands/feet.
o Forest Bathing (Shinrin-yoku): Spending time among trees (Japanese
practice). Reduces mental clarity and stress. Breathe deeply, empty the
mind. Experience absorbing energy from the tree.
o Dream Connection: Strengthen connection through dreams. Before sleep,
intend to talk to the tree. May receive messages or experiences in dreams or
afterwards.
• How

on: Strengthen connection through dreams. Before sleep,
intend to talk to the tree. May receive messages or experiences in dreams or
afterwards.
• How trees communicate: Trees talk in signals, not words. A monk who chanted
under a Banyan tree for 12 years dreamt the tree said, "You reside within me, now I
will speak." After that, the monk started speaking as if the tree was his Guru.
• Scientific support (mentioned in sources): Suzanne Simard's research (Mother
Tree theory) suggests trees communicate via mycorrhizal networks. Plant
Neurobiology suggests trees feel pain, remember, and react. Kirlian photography
shows trees have auras that change when spoken to, confirming they are living and

conscious. Trees absorb emotional energy. Each tree has a personality. Forest
Bathing is a Japanese therapy reducing stress; not just air changes, but the whole
thought process.
• Sadhana Plan Example (21 days): Lists specific trees and mantras for daily
practice over 5 days, with suggested purposes (body purification, inner self,
emotional clarity, knowledge, communication).
• Essence: Vanaspati Tantra is an act of love towards nature. Trees speak when you
learn to listen, when you become quiet, echoing your soul's resonance.
11. Shakti Sadhana (General)
• Shakti Sadhana is the practice of invoking Shakti in the form of a Goddess,
specifically Maa Durga, Kali, and Lakshmi.
• Its purpose is to awaken the hidden energy and power within a person.
• It establishes balance in self-confidence, mental strength, and various aspects of
life.
• It is a deep inner journey, a unique way to connect with the invisible world of the
soul and Brahma. It develops physical and mental strength and fills life with new
perspective and power.
• More than an external process, it awakens inner power. Sadhak feels deep peace
and elemental consciousness, leading towards self-realization and understanding
tr

n external process, it awakens inner power. Sadhak feels deep peace
and elemental consciousness, leading towards self-realization and understanding
true nature. It connects to the supreme truth of Brahma.
• Worship of Maa Durga awakens extraordinary power for facing life's struggles and
guides towards union with Brahma. Worship of Kali gives mental courage and inner
strength for life's difficulties. Worship of Lakshmi leads to economic prosperity and
reduces mental stress/worry.
• It establishes harmony in body and mind, giving strength to overcome any crisis.
• Sadhak experiences unseen inner power, becomes self-sufficient, not needing
external resources for life's difficulties; inner power revitalizes them. It makes them
mentally strong, able to overcome any challenge.
• Surrendering to divine powers makes them feel no problem is too big with inner
strength and faith. Experiences power in every aspect of life (physical health, mental
state, social relations).
• Each form of Shakti Sadhana provides inner power and guidance towards balance
and success in every aspect of life. It elevates the spiritual journey.
• Forms discussed:
o Maa Durga Worship: Symbol of power, courage, strugg

lance
and success in every aspect of life. It elevates the spiritual journey.
• Forms discussed:
o Maa Durga Worship: Symbol of power, courage, struggle, protector of the
cosmos, destroys evil/unrighteousness. Gives courage and strength for life's
challenges. Involves Durga Saptashati recitation, Durga Kavach chanting,
special fasts. Awakens unique power, provides mental and physical strength.
Inner powers awaken, ready to face difficulties. Provides confidence and
inner balance.
o Maa Kali Worship: Deep and fierce form of power. Goddess of destruction
and transformation, also ends negative energy within. Worship especially for

removing mental and physical obstacles. Involves Vama Mantra Jap, Tantra
Sadhana, and special Pujas. Embodies fierce power and inner courage.
Sadhak overcomes fear and negative thoughts. Not just increases physical
strength, but removes mental/inner obstacles. Chanting Kali's mantras helps
overcome negativity and emerge stronger for life's struggles. Facing Kali's
power, Sadhak feels the free form of the soul is their greatest strength.
o Maa Lakshmi Worship: Goddess of wealth, prosperity, opulence. Blessings
bring happiness, peace, and prosperity. Worship brings wealth and opulence
in every aspect of life. Main goal is obtaining wealth and prosperity. Involves
Shri Sukta recitation (considered very effective). Opens path to material
prosperity, promotes mental peace and balance. Sadhak understands
prosperity comes from mental peace/balance as much as wealth. Life is fully
prosperous when both exist.
• Method (General): A deep and mindful process requiring discipline and dedication.
o Location: Peaceful and sacred place free from distractions. Can be a
specific corner at home designated as a worship area.
o Mantra Jap: Mantras have power to bring peace, energy, and Goddess's
blessi

ractions. Can be a
specific corner at home designated as a worship area.
o Mantra Jap: Mantras have power to bring peace, energy, and Goddess's
blessings. Examples: Durga (Om Dum Durgayai Namah), Kali (Om Kleem Kali
Maha Krur Maha Kruri Maha Krur Namah), Lakshmi (Om Shri Mahalakshmyai
Namah). Chanting purifies mind, body, soul, attracts grace, awakens inner
energy, gives strength, courage, mental clarity. Pronunciation must be
correct for power to work properly. Gives mental peace and divine power
sensation. Fills Sadhak with confidence to face difficulties.
o Mudras and Tantra Sadhana: Important aspect of Shakti Sadhana. Goal is to
control and awaken inner energy. Special Mudras establish balance in
body/mind, increasing Sadhana power. Tantra Sadhana involves special
mantras and actions to effectively bring Goddess's powers into life. Sadhak
gathers energy of mind and body to bring blessings into life. Sitting in special
Mudra increases peace and concentration, focusing energy. Mudras and
Tantra Sadhana strengthen from within, giving experience of inner power.
o Vrat (Fasting) and Upavasan (Abstinence): Performed during Shakti
Sadhana to strengthen self-control and austerity. Fast

experience of inner power.
o Vrat (Fasting) and Upavasan (Abstinence): Performed during Shakti
Sadhana to strengthen self-control and austerity. Fasting is a form of
austerity inspiring restrained life and awakening inner power. Abstinence
involves controlling diet and routine for mental/physical purity and energy.
Purifies mind/soul, allowing reception of blessings with faith. Purifies body,
speeds up spiritual journey. Austerity and fasting lead to mental strength,
physical health, spiritual development.
• Benefits: Spiritual progress and grace, brings balance, power, peace in every aspect
of life. Significant positive changes. Increases self-confidence and mental strength
greatly. Overcomes fear, hesitation, mental blocks. Makes Sadhak strong and self-
sufficient to achieve goals. No obstacle exists with mental stability and confidence.
Breaks limitations, realises true potential, steps towards dreams. Gives power to
control mental state for facing difficulties.

• Physical Effects: Removes physical weakness, makes person healthy and
energetic. Awakening inner power affects physical health. Infuses body with energy,
removes mental fatigue, makes physically active. Physical practices and meditation
improve blood circulation, energizing every part. Increases flow of positive energy,
Sadhak feels fresh and energetic daily.
• Effect on Others: Impacts not just the Sadhak but those around them. Inner power
and positive energy influence others. Sadhak can inspire and guide as inner strength
enables showing light to others. Brings positive energy flow, influencing
surroundings. Dedicated Sadhak's life becomes joyful and peaceful. Inner
peace/balance protects from external negative influences. Gives mental clarity,
peace, contentment. Faces struggles with confidence/courage. Progresses towards
purpose, surrounding people feel the energy.
12. Durga Saptashati Yantra Sadhana
• Durga Saptashati Yantra Sadhana is described as very powerful and effective,
granting divine power and experience.
• The Durga Saptashati Yantra is not ordinary, but the symbol of Maa Durga's
conscious energy. It attracts unseen powers, removes life's crises, and grants
extraor

aptashati Yantra is not ordinary, but the symbol of Maa Durga's
conscious energy. It attracts unseen powers, removes life's crises, and grants
extraordinary Siddhis. Its power is immense, capable of bringing wonderful changes
if Sadhan is done correctly.
• Mentioned as a Sadhana where results are seen. Adherence to rules is essential,
otherwise, harm can occur.
• Considered extremely influential and fierce (Ugra). Only for those with full faith and
dedication towards Maa Durga. Those wanting to just "try it out" should stay away.
• Rules: Strict observance of Brahmacharya, no lies or deceit, do not cut hair or
beard, mandatory bath after excretion, use lamp of pure cow ghee, rose-scented
incense, wear red clothes, sit on a red mat, place a powerful and Siddha picture of
Maa Durga at the worship place, face East, use Rudraksha mala for Jap, offer dry
fruits and rose flower daily, start Sadhana at night after 9 PM.
• Timing: Can be started from the 8th day (Ashtami) of the dark half (Krishna Paksha)
of any month until Amavasya.
• Duration: Daily 11 malas of mantra jap is mandatory for the entire Sadhana period.
• Yantra Creation: Must be made before starting Sadhana. Prepare on Bhoj

on: Daily 11 malas of mantra jap is mandatory for the entire Sadhana period.
• Yantra Creation: Must be made before starting Sadhana. Prepare on Bhoj Patra
(parchment) using Asthagandha ink and a pomegranate pen. Install Yantra at
Sadhana place and worship daily.
• Havan: Necessary on the final day. Use black sesame seeds and ghee as material.
Offer 1/10th of the total mantra jap count as oblations (Dashansh Aahuti). After
Havan, offer pomegranate sacrifice and cosmetic items ( श्ंगर ार सामग्री) to the Mother.
• Benefits: Functions as a protective shield (Raksha Kavach). Protects from negative
energy, evil eye, Tantric effects, and enemy obstacles. Protects from evil in the
current competitive/negative environment. Fills Sadhak with positive energy. Can
fulfill all wishes (wealth, success, health, spiritual progress). Helps Sadhak reach
their goals. Life becomes happy and prosperous by Maa Durga's grace. Awakens

inherent powers. Fills with spiritual energy, gives divine experiences. Increases
mental strength, understanding, confidence to face challenges. Acts as a protective
shield against untimely death, accidents, or unknown fear. Frees from fear, provides
divine protection. Makes Sadhak physically and mentally strong. Increases
confidence to face any situation. Provides inner strength for success at every turn.
• Location: Establish the Yantra at home or workplace. Keep Yantra in a clean and
sacred place.
• Practice: Worship regularly, chant Durga Saptashati mantras. Wearing the Yantra
also brings benefits.
• Warning: Do not perform any Sadhana described on the channel without the
guidance of a qualified Guru. Sadhana is serious and improper practice can cause
heavy harm. It is only for those with an experienced Guru. If you don't have a Guru,
you can still watch to gain knowledge.
I have expanded on each Tantra and Tantric practice mentioned in the sources, drawing
information only from the provided texts and citing each point.
